Industry Mar 21, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Advanced operational optimization technologies can simultaneously increase airport capacity and reduce aviation's environmental footprint as traffic grows.

Iran has launched two missiles at the joint UK-U.S. base at Diego Garcia in th...

Strategic ballistic missile demonstration over the Indian Ocean signals expanded threat range to critical military aviation infrastructure and allied air operations.

NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight

NASA’s quiet supersonic X-59 aircraft made its second flight on Friday, kicking off a series of dozens of test flights in 2026. NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic technology advances toward commercial viability, with 2026 test campaign positioning acoustic innovation as key differentiator for future supersonic routes.

FlightPath3D's cinematic moving map technology has achieved significant market penetration in business aviation, validating demand for advanced cockpit visualization solutions.

Regulatory Mar 20, 2026

A photojournalist is challenging FAA’s drone ban over DHS vehicles

The FAA is being challenged on a recent flight restriction that banned drone flights over all government buildings and mobile assets. The lawsuit is being managed by The Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press and is challenging it on both First Amendment and Administrative Procedure Act vio...

FAA's blanket drone restrictions over DHS assets face First Amendment legal challenge, potentially reshaping press freedom in aerial journalism.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain

The contract covers the design and construction of an opti...

Laser communication ground station technology developed for space applications may enable next-generation aircraft-to-satellite connectivity for aviation operations.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call

NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator experienced an unscheduled early return during its second test flight, potentially impacting the timeline for validating low-boom technology critical to future supersonic civil aviation.

DJI's pricing strategy for the Flip targets mass-market adoption by reducing both cost and user friction, expanding the consumer drone market.

How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries

The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS will soon leave our solar system, never to return, but the observations of the comet will live on in NASA’s public data archives. More than a dozen NASA science missions turned their instruments to observe the comet, which is only the third identified object to be ...

NASA's open-source comet observation data demonstrates how public aerospace research archives accelerate scientific discovery beyond initial missions.
